title: Building a Small Date library

details that in maintenance mode and they recommend against using it projects seems like lot of people turn dayjs as an alternative lightweight has nearly identical apis i am process rewriting my setdate setyear both there endof might guessed bring end beginning particular unit used here behind scenes calls rather than separate subtract single manipulate takes integer positive negative plus affect formatting features way define what acceptable users formatted select dates now provides through me feel clean mm dd yyyy d c requires template always produce expected result following will rz isbefore isafter isbetween issame moments except course heavy lifting february pm false true since expecting results created return flat parts 'en' year numeric formattoparts type value __proto__ cleaner information shortcuts various getseconds setseconds instead seconds other bonus week couple monthformatted digit does shift had whose idea github play stackblitz open source
excerpt: I created a small date library for javascript using native function and no external dependencies as a replacement for needing moment.js or day.js.
tags: date, javascript, open source, time, moment.js, day.js
